verificationLabel: Round 10 team lists checked Thursday 7 May 2026
summary: Round 10 starts tonight as a reset week after the 4 from 8 stumble in Round 9, with Sharks head to head shaping as the most interesting early charity value but no bet locked yet.

scoreboard.note: Canterbury get key names back but still arrive off a confidence-hit loss, while the Dolphins stay the cleaner favourite at Suncorp.
The Roosters price is cramped but still looks too short for charity and too strong to fade in tipping.
North Queensland remain one of the better home reads in the comp and Parramatta have not done enough to force an upset call.
Newcastle still look one of the cleaner reads of the week with Ponga steering a healthier spine against the winless Dragons.
This is the most interesting price on the board: Souths lose Jack Wighton, Cronulla are settled, and the number finally pays for the risk.
Brisbane lose more shape without Reynolds, Mariner and Staggs, while Manly get a structural lift with Jamal Fogarty back.
Jahrome Hughes returns and the market still favours Melbourne, but this remains a trust test more than a comfort pick after seven straight losses.
Penrith stay in the reliability bucket until proven otherwise, even away from home.

liveAlerts.text: Round 10 opens tonight with a clean reset chance after the 4 from 8 wobble in Round 9.
The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger stays at 2-2 and -$3.50, so discipline matters more than trying to win it all back in one bet.
Sharks head to head at around $2.25 is the first charity number this week that looks like proper value rather than a token underdog flyer.
Broncos have been downgraded by the outs to Reynolds, Mariner and Staggs, while Manly get a boost with Fogarty back.
Storm still sit in the no-trust bucket until a result says otherwise, even with Hughes returning.
No Round 10 charity bet is locked yet, which is the right call until one last late-mail sanity check.
